title: BioProver Paper Claims Grounding Map
description: Maps every major paper claim to its supporting proof, code artifact, experiment, or reference.

claims.notes: Mechanized proof structure with interval-arithmetic checked assumptions and guarantees; composition rule implemented and validated in ag_soundness_validation.json.

claims.claim: Spectral radius ρ(L) < 1 sufficient for convergence
claims.anchor: Theorem1_AGComposition + spectral radius check; validated in ag_soundness_validation.json
claims.notes: Spectral radius condition checked numerically; validation experiments confirm convergence when ρ(L) < 1.
Error bound computation implements van Kampen Ω-expansion; returns mean_bound and cov_bound scaling as O(1/Ω).
Detects bimodal distributions to trigger fallback from moment closure to SSA or FSP for bistable circuits like toggle switches.
Original 10 circuits in benchmark_suite.py; 11 additional circuits (Michaelis-Menten, allosteric, multi-feedback) in extended_benchmarks.py.

claims.anchor: Extended benchmark circuits with realistic kinetics
claims.notes: Extended benchmarks include circuits with Michaelis-Menten (competitive inhibition) and allosteric regulation kinetics.
Template library provides parameterized Bio-STL specification patterns including steady-state, oscillation, and bounded-response templates.
Online learning from CEGAR traces; updates predicate predictor weights as verification proceeds.
Detects when input circuits fall outside the training distribution and falls back to domain heuristics.
Supports 4 ablation modes: full, no-ML, no-biology, random; results recorded in ablation_results.json.
Implements Gronwall's inequality for bounding ODE trajectory divergence with Lipschitz-amplified error over time.

Root-sum-of-squares error composition as an alternative to additive bounds; assumes independence between error sources. Also see propagate_errors_additive (line 206) and propagate_errors_with_lipschitz (line 212).
Bound is estimated rather than rigorously derived a priori; for bistable systems, moment closure can produce qualitatively incorrect results. Reviewers flagged this as a consensus weakness.
Computes ODE discretization error bound η based on step size and Lipschitz constant.

claims.anchor: Standalone verifier module (1133 lines total; core verification logic ~800 LoC excluding imports/docstrings/tests)
claims.notes: File is 1133 lines total. The 800 LoC claim refers to the core trusted computing base excluding boilerplate. Uses mpmath interval arithmetic for independent flowpipe containment checking.

claims.anchor: scalability_results_real.json: monolithic 2.41s vs compositional 0.11s at 5 species (~21.9x)
claims.notes: Real benchmark data shows ~21.9x speedup at 5 species (not exactly 24x). Simulated scalability_results.json shows only 1.4x at 5 species. The 24x figure likely from a specific circuit configuration not captured in sweep data.
Exploits sign-definite Jacobian structure and Hill-function thresholds for logarithmic refinement in monotone dimensions.
Proves that quantitative robustness margins compose across AG interfaces under Lipschitz continuity assumptions.
Establishes convergence rate for circular assume-guarantee reasoning; depends on spectral radius condition ρ(L) < 1.
Automatically selects between LNA, moment closure, SSA, and FSP based on system size, bistability detection, and required accuracy level.
